name: Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop
name_original: Pico dos Tres Bispos - circular desde San Román de Cervantes
description: The Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop is a difficult 11.8-mile jogging route in Serra dos Ancares, offering panoramic mountain views from its summit
region_intro: Jogging around Cervantes offers challenging routes through mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes. The region is characterized by natural paths and trails that wind through varied landscapes. These running routes provide opportunities to experience the area's rugged beauty and demanding ascents. The trails are generally suited for runners seeking a more strenuous workout. Best jogging routes around Cervantes The most popular jogging route is Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop, an 11.8 miles (19.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete. This route features substantial elevation gain, typical of the region's mountainous paths. Another top favourite among local runners is Church of San Xoán de Hospital – Ancient Chestnut Tree in Ramil loop from Padornelo, a difficult 26.8 miles (43.1 km) path. This extensive route explores diverse natural scenery, including historical landmarks and ancient trees. Local runners also love the Pico O Mostallar loop from ZEPA Ancares, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 10 minutes. Jogging around Cervantes is defined by mountainous terrain, significant elevation gains, and natural trails. The network offers options primarily for experienced runners seeking challenging routes. The routes in Cervantes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 50 runners have used komoot to explore Cervantes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes in Cervantes?
A: Running routes in Cervantes are characterized by &lt;strong&gt;mountainous terrain&lt;/strong&gt; with significant elevation changes. You'll primarily find natural paths and trails, making them suitable for runners seeking a more strenuous workout and challenging ascents.

Q: How many jogging routes are available in Cervantes?
A: There are 5 dedicated jogging routes around Cervantes featured in this guide. All of them are rated as difficult, offering a challenging experience for experienced runners.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running trails in Cervantes?
A: The running routes around Cervantes are generally designed for experienced runners, featuring significant elevation gains and difficult terrain. There are no easy or moderate routes listed in this guide; all are rated as difficult.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I might see while jogging in Cervantes?
A: While jogging in Cervantes, especially on routes that traverse the Ancares region, you might encounter historical and natural landmarks. These include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/383799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Real in O Cebreiro&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/716033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrim Statue at Alto de San Roque&lt;/a&gt;, and various mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/79543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The three passes of O Cebreiro&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views and a sense of the region's rich history.

Q: What is the longest running route in Cervantes?
A: The longest running route in Cervantes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40302095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Xoán de Hospital – Ancient Chestnut Tree in Ramil loop from Padornelo&lt;/a&gt;. This extensive path covers 43.1 km (26.8 miles) and features substantial elevation gain, exploring diverse natural scenery and historical landmarks.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Cervantes?
A: The running routes in Cervantes are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. Over 50 runners have used komoot to explore the varied and challenging terrain, appreciating the region's rugged beauty.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Cervantes?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Cervantes are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991245866/pico-dos-tres-bispos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24566750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico O Mostallar loop from ZEPA Ancares&lt;/a&gt; are both excellent options for a loop run.

Q: What is the shortest challenging running route in Cervantes?
A: For a shorter but still difficult run, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brañutín – Cuíña loop from Porto de Ancares&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 6.8 km (4.2 miles) long and involves significant elevation gain, making it a compact yet demanding option.

Q: What kind of natural scenery can I expect on the running trails?
A: The running trails in Cervantes offer breathtaking natural scenery, characterized by the mountainous landscapes of the Ancares region. You'll experience rolling hills, verdant valleys, and expansive views, especially on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancares Mountains – Pico dos Tres Bispos loop from A Campa de Fieiró&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Cervantes a good destination for trail running?
A: Absolutely. Cervantes is an excellent destination for trail running due to its mountainous terrain, natural paths, and significant elevation changes. The routes are well-suited for those who enjoy challenging off-road running experiences amidst impressive scenery.

Q: What is the typical duration for completing a running route in Cervantes?
A: The duration varies significantly depending on the route's length and your fitness level. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991245866/pico-dos-tres-bispos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, at 19.0 km (11.8 miles), typically takes around 2 hours 36 minutes, while longer routes can take much more time.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Ancares Mountains?
A: Yes, many routes in Cervantes provide stunning views of the Ancares Mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancares Mountains – Pico dos Tres Bispos loop from A Campa de Fieiró&lt;/a&gt; is specifically named for this region, offering an immersive experience in the heart of the mountains.
tips: At an altitude of 1,794 meters, at the point where Galicia delimits Castilla and León, the top of Pico dos Tres Bispos offers beautiful views over the Serra dos Ancares.
Free refuge located between the peaks of Tres Bispos and Penarrubia. Its doors are always open. The space has bunk beds, a fireplace and other elements for cooking.

faqs.question: Is the Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop suitable for jogging?
faqs.answer: While the trail is classified for jogging, it is considered difficult due to its challenging terrain and significant elevation changes. It features long, continuous ascents, especially towards the summit of Pico dos Tres Bispos. This route is generally more suitable for experienced trail runners with good physical condition and sure-footedness, or for hiking.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding. You will encounter comfortable forest tracks in some sections, but also narrow, steep, and potentially uncomfortable single-track ascents, particularly as you approach the summit. After rain, some parts can be muddy and have puddles. Appropriate footwear and potentially walking poles are highly recommended.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the loop?
faqs.answer: For an experienced trail runner, the estimated duration is around 2.6 hours. However, given the trail's difficult rating and challenging terrain, hikers or those jogging at a more moderate pace should allow significantly more time, potentially 5-7 hours, to comfortably complete the nearly 19 km route with its 779 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The loop typically starts from San Román de Cervantes. Look for parking options within the village or at designated areas near the trailhead. Specific directions are best confirmed with local resources or a detailed map before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally dog-friendly, but given its challenging nature, ensure your dog is fit enough for a long, strenuous route with significant elevation changes. Always keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and respect other trail users, especially as the area is part of a Biosphere Reserve.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit?
faqs.answer: The best time to experience the Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop is typically during the late spring, summer, and early autumn months (May to October). During these periods, the weather is generally more stable, and the diverse flora, including heather and broom, is in bloom. Winter can bring snow and ice, making the trail significantly more challenging and potentially impassable.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike or jog the Pico dos Tres Bispos Loop. The trail is located within the Reserva da Biosfera Os Ancares Lucenses e Montes de Cervantes, Navia e Becerreá, which is a protected natural area. While there are no fees, visitors are expected to follow responsible outdoor ethics to preserve the environment.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers impressive and breathtaking scenery throughout. You'll be treated to panoramic 360-degree views of the Serra dos Ancares mountain range from the summit of Pico dos Tres Bispos. The landscape is rich with diverse flora like oak, hazel, birch, and holly, and you might spot wildlife such as deer, wild boars, or various bird species. You'll also pass by the Brego Shelter.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, pack essentials such as sturdy hiking or trail running shoes, plenty of water, high-energy snacks, and appropriate layers of clothing for changing mountain weather. A map or GPS device is recommended, and walking poles can be very helpful for the steep ascents and descents. Don't forget sun protection and a first-aid kit.

faqs.question: Are there any accommodations or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The village of San Román de Cervantes, where the trail typically starts, offers some basic amenities. For more extensive options, you might look into nearby rural accommodations or small guesthouses. The Brego Shelter is a hut along the route, but it's primarily for emergency or basic overnight stays, not a full-service accommodation.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see in the Serra dos Ancares region?
faqs.answer: The Serra dos Ancares is home to a rich variety of wildlife. While sightings are never guaranteed, you might encounter deer, wild boars, foxes, and martens. The area is also known for its birdlife, including goshawks and eagles. Keep a respectful distance and observe from afar to avoid disturbing these animals in their natural habitat.
